children.value: This notebook follows the model presented in section four of “Regional Consumption Responses and the Aggregate Fiscal Multiplier”
. Here we abstract from the behavior of the intermediate firms and regional mutual funds in each region, treating them as exogenous to the households.
This document shows how to decompose one period of the household problem into a sequence of modular stages.
Households maximize lifetime utility by choosing consumption and labor supply.

children.value: They are subject to idiosyncratic productivity shocks, receive rate of return on assets saved in a regional mutual fund (risky return), and dividends from intermediate good firms that depend on the average productivity of workers in the region.
